human_verification.notes: Reviewed source discipline, boundary safety, protected-record handling, community-care specificity, and evidence-traceability expectations for reporting and disclosure scenarios.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest defines explicit community-care reporting tasks, protected-record controls, and adjacent-lane refusals.
Research summary links behavioral-health reporting, community-care quality, survey, and privacy sources to workflow stages and failure modes.
Functionality map covers shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry abilities with full scenario coverage.
Evaluation scenarios explicitly test ambiguous metrics, stale or conflicting data, unsupported claims, missing submission inputs, tenant overrides, and industry-specific routing.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were introduced in this package iteration.
Scenario coverage is complete and aligns with issue-required families and workflow families.

notes: This package is intended to be validated as a specification-based specialist lane with explicit escalation and refusal behavior.
Next step for retained-implementation runtime is a separate, reviewed artifact pipeline promotion.
